clinical.chief_complaints: Recurrent right upper quadrant pain after fatty meals for 8 months. Painless swelling at navel since 2 years, increasing in size, reducible, no bowel complaints.
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Patient is a 48-year-old male who has been experiencing episodic right upper quadrant and epigastric pain for 8 months, typically occurring 30-60 minutes after meals, especially fatty food. Two episodes of severe biliary colic in the past 3 months. No jaundice, no fever. Separately, patient noticed a swelling at the umbilicus 2 years ago, initially small and reducible. No pain at umbilical swelling site. No bowel obstruction symptoms. Both conditions evaluated simultaneously and patient planned for combined surgery under single anaesthesia.
clinical.planned_procedure: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy with simultaneous open umbilical hernia repair (mesh)

examination_findings.general: Conscious, oriented, mild central obesity, no pallor, no icterus, no lymphadenopathy
examination_findings.cvs: S1 S2 heard, no murmurs, regular rate and rhythm
examination_findings.rs: Bilateral air entry clear, no added sounds
examination_findings.abdomen: Soft, mild tenderness in right hypochondrium on deep palpation. Murphy's sign positive. Umbilical swelling 3x3 cm, reducible, non-tender, positive cough impulse, no overlying skin changes.
examination_findings.local: Umbilical region: reducible hernia with defect palpable approximately 2 cm, bowel sounds present in sac

investigations.result_summary: USG abdomen: Multiple calculi in gallbladder, largest 12mm. Gallbladder wall mildly thickened at 4mm. No common bile duct dilation. CBD 4mm. Liver normal. Umbilical hernia with small bowel loops in sac confirmed.
LFT: Total bilirubin 0.8 mg/dL, direct 0.2, SGOT 32, SGPT 38, ALP 88. CBC: Hb 13.2, WBC 9100, platelets 2.6 lakh. RFT: Creatinine 0.9. PT/INR 1.1. Blood glucose fasting 104 mg/dL.

clinical.past_medical_history: No significant past medical history. No prior abdominal surgeries.

non_clinical_documents_in_hand.label: Photo of patient on hospital bed

clinical.notes: Patient fit for combined surgery. Pre-anaesthetic check cleared. Planned for laparoscopic cholecystectomy followed by umbilical hernia mesh repair under same general anaesthesia. Informed consent obtained for both procedures.
